rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ROBERT L. KEARNS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/2020. 1) Paragraph 4b(i) alleges that Florence Passamanno received the annuity funds upon the conservatee's death, but according to Schedules B & G, the annuity ceased to exist after 05/29/2018. 2) Paragraph 5 alleges that the current bond amount is $46,000, but the order approving the second account reduced the bond to $53,000. 3) Paragraph 9 alleges that the conservatee executed a statutory will. Did the conservatee have capacity to do so? 4) Paragraph 9 alleges that the estate holds 25 shares of Metlife, but Schedules E & F show 26 shares. 5) Schedule G lists the annuity account 3074. Did this account exist during this account period? 6) Florence Passamanno's original small estate affidavit must be filed. 7) A copy of the petition must be given to the VA. The proof of service shows only notice was given to the VA.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF HAJRA N. KHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/2020. 1) No original account statements have been filed. 2) The bond calculation in paragraph 6 appears incorrect. An ex parte petition for the correct bond increase must be filed. 3) Schedule C shows some expenses of the trust. Why were they paid from the conservatorship estate? 4) Please provide a copy of the attorney's invoice for the $2,125 payment on 11/19/2019. 4) As to paragraph 10, please address Probate Code section 2582. 5) As to Exhibit A, should the petitioner's time spent on conflict waivers be compensated by the conservatorship estate? 6) As to Exhibit C: i) Should ME's time (0.3 hours) for the sole purpose of requesting a continuance on 03/10/2020 be compensated by the conservatorship estate? ii) The paralegal appears to have spent many hours on the inventory and appraisal. Was the time productively spent? iii) Should the paralegal's work on "ex parte petition for authority to open POD account" on 12/10/2019 & 12/12/2019 be compensated, even though no ex parte was filed?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ABDUL AZIZ KHAN AND HAJRA NIGHAT KHAN JOINT LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner or other evidence addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) Some pages are missing from the Schwab account statement for November 2019 (filed on 10/13/2020). 2) Has the Exemption Trust been created? 3) Have the reported assets been allocated between the Survivor's Trust and Marital Trust? 4) Paragraphs 7 & 8 of the petition refer to the conservator. 5) Do Yasmeen Khan, Shehla Khan, and Rehan Khan share expenses for the trust real properties? 6) According to the trust amendment, Rehan Kahn is entitled to be reimbursed from the trust residue for the cost of improvements to the Olmstead property. Should this be characterized as a liability of the trust and disclosed on Schedule I? 7) There are no additional schedules for the Mangels and Olmstead properties for which specific beneficiaries have been designated. Probate Code section 1063(d). 8) As to Exhibit C: i) Should ME's time (0.3 hours) for the sole purpose of requesting a continuance on 03/10/2020 be compensated by the trust? ii) The paralegals appear to have spent more than 10 hours on the inventory and appraisal. Was the time productively spent?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARCIA ANN GRUNOW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues were e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FIRST INITIAL AND LAST NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 6th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Freedman said he would be giving notice and Friedeberg said she would be filing objections at the 09/01/2020 hearing. 1) Competing Petitions to Probate Wills. a. The court to take a first look at the pleadings and discuss the best way to manage these petitions. b. William filed objections and a will contest in response to Matthew's petition. c. Matthew has not filed objections or a will contest in response to William's petition. 2) The Court to determine whether to grant the petition.

(Probate Code 17002). 2) Please state the names and last known addresses of all vested and contingent beneficiaries. (CRC 7.902). 3) Please file copies of the Trusts. 4) Adrianne E. Vincent has not been personally served.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAM HOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/2020. 1) No proof of a subscribing witness to the 05/14/2019 will is on file. The will is not self-proving as the witnesses did not sign under penalty of perjury. 2) No proof of publication is on file. 3) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with William Sriwiset Hom must be identified and receive separate notice. CRC 7.51(d). 4) The petition requests a bond waiver, but who is authorized to waive the bond on behalf of William Sriwiset Hom? 5) Are Pawinee Sriwiset and William Sriwiset Hom Thai citizens? If so, notice must be given to a Thai Consulate. Probate Code section 8113.
